summary: chain-key-validator ships wallet/credential scanner with hardcoded C2
details: The package contains three reachable modules — wallet-scanner.js, scanner.js, and build-verifier.js — that together implement a credential and wallet stealer with a hardcoded exfiltration channel. scanner.js posts harvested system intel (os.hostname(), whoami, execSync output) to a hardcoded GitHub Pages C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 143, 161, 323) and uses curl-based commands at lines 298/323/408 to fetch and run further content. wallet-scanner.js performs the wallet-extension/credential-harvest fingerprint with multiple POSTs and child_process exec paths. build-verifier.js layers in additional os/fs/https/child_process exfiltration plumbing, including an execSync('docker push...') path at line 210. The package's stated purpose ("chain-key-validator") does not justify scanning the host, executing curl/whoami, reading filesystem state, or beaconing to an attacker-controlled github.io page. Installer harm: any environment that installs and loads this package leaks host identity and locally-readable secrets/wallet artifacts to the operator of ddjidd564.github.io and may execute attacker-fetched commands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
